site.title: NewsPub
site.footer: NewsPub turns source-attributed news into website and social publishing workflows.

common.emptyStateDescription: Published stories will appear here once connected streams begin releasing content.

common.shareDescription: Send this story to teammates or publish it through your own channels.

home.title: Source-based news, published clearly.
home.description: Browse approved stories curated from trusted sources and organized for fast reading.
home.metaDescription: Browse approved NewsPub stories, categories, and recent coverage.

news.title: Published stories
news.description: Browse the latest published website stories from NewsPub's ingestion and review pipeline.
news.metaDescription: Read the latest published NewsPub website stories.

search.title: Search published stories
search.description: Search the public NewsPub website for published stories, source names, and category pages.

about.title: About NewsPub
about.description: NewsPub is a reuse-first publishing system for ingesting third-party news, reviewing canonical story artifacts, and publishing approved output to the website and social destinations.
about.metaDescription: Learn how NewsPub ingests, reviews, and publishes source-attributed news stories.

sections.title: What NewsPub does
paragraphs: NewsPub consumes supported third-party news APIs, normalizes provider payloads into a shared article contract, filters them against destination stream rules, and stores only publishable or published records.
Editors work from canonical story artifacts so the website, Facebook, and Instagram can all publish from the same approved source-backed record.

sections.title: Release 1 boundaries
items: Release 1 is not an AI writing product.
Provider credentials stay in environment variables and are never entered through the dashboard.
Only published website stories appear on public routes.
paragraphs: The public site stays locale-ready from the start, with English active by default.

privacy.title: Privacy policy
privacy.description: The public website is focused on reading published stories while analytics and audit boundaries stay explicit from the start.

sections.title: Public analytics
paragraphs: NewsPub records lightweight website view analytics to understand traffic patterns across home, category, search, and story pages.
The public site does not expose provider secrets, destination tokens, or raw internal operational payloads.

sections.title: Admin and audit boundaries
items: Operational audit events are kept in protected admin workflows only.
Destination tokens are encrypted before persistence.
paragraphs: Release 1 keeps admin and public concerns separated so public browsing never requires a login.

disclaimer.title: Disclaimer
disclaimer.description: NewsPub publishes source-attributed stories for informational use and preserves visible attribution, timestamps, and editorial review state.
disclaimer.metaDescription: Read the NewsPub public publishing disclaimer.

sections.title: Source attribution matters
paragraphs: Published stories summarize and reformat third-party source material for the configured NewsPub destinations.
Readers should review the linked source and original publisher when accuracy, policy, legal, or operational decisions depend on exact wording.

sections.title: Editorial and platform boundaries
items: Release 1 does not invent facts or generate new reporting.
Platform-specific formatting does not change the underlying source facts.
Operational logs and retry behavior remain visible to authenticated admins.
